examples.title: Fetch-Execute Cycle Microoperations
examples.description: Pseudocode representing the microoperations performed during the fetch and execute phases of instruction processing.
examples.code:
// FETCH PHASE (same for all instructions)
MAR ← PC // Load address from PC
ReadMem(MAR) // Memory read operation
IR ← MDR // Instruction into IR
PC ← PC + 4 // Increment PC (word-aligned)

// EXECUTE PHASE (depends on instruction type)

// R-type: ADD Rdest, Rsrc1, Rsrc2
ALUInput1 ← R[src1] // Read source registers
ALUInput2 ← R[src2]
ALUResult ← ALUInput1 + ALUInput2 // Perform addition
R[dest] ← ALUResult // Write result

// Load: LW Rdest, offset(Rsrc)
ALUResult ← R[src] + offset // Compute effective address
MAR ← ALUResult
ReadMem(MAR) // Memory read
R[dest] ← MDR // Load data into register

// Store: SW Rsrc, offset(Rdest)
ALUResult ← R[dest] + offset // Compute address
MAR ← ALUResult
MDR ← R[src] // Data to store
WriteMem(MAR) // Memory write
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the register transfer microoperations. Each line represents an atomic operation that can complete in one clock cycle. The fetch phase is universal; execute phase varies by instruction type as determined by the opcode field in IR.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - uses fixed set of internal registers

examples.title: Pipeline Hazard Detection and Forwarding Logic
examples.description: Pseudocode showing how the pipeline detects data hazards and implements forwarding to resolve RAW dependencies.
examples.code:
// Forwarding Unit Logic (in EX stage)
if (EX/MEM.RegWrite AND // Previous instruction writes register
 EX/MEM.RegisterRd != 0 AND // Not $zero
 EX/MEM.RegisterRd == ID/EX.RegisterRs) then
 ForwardA = 10 // Forward from EX/MEM pipeline register

if (EX/MEM.RegWrite AND 
 EX/MEM.RegisterRd != 0 AND
 EX/MEM.RegisterRd == ID/EX.RegisterRt) then
 ForwardB = 10 // Forward from EX/MEM

// Also check MEM/WB for forwarding
if (MEM/WB.RegWrite AND 
 MEM/WB.RegisterRd != 0 AND
 MEM/WB.RegisterRd == ID/EX.RegisterRs AND
 NOT (EX/MEM.RegWrite AND EX/MEM.RegisterRd == ID/EX.RegisterRs)) then
 ForwardA = 01 // Forward from MEM/WB

// Apply forwarding to ALU inputs
ALUInput1 = (ForwardA == 00) ? ID/EX.RegisterRs :
 (ForwardA == 10) ? EX/MEM.ALUResult :
 MEM/WB.WriteData

ALUInput2 = (ForwardB == 00) ? ID/EX.RegisterRt :
 (ForwardB == 10) ? EX/MEM.ALUResult :
 MEM/WB.WriteData
examples.explanation: This forwarding logic detects when the source registers of the current instruction match the destination registers of previously in-flight instructions. The 2-bit ForwardA/ForwardB signals select whether to use the original register value or forwarded data from either the EX/MEM or MEM/WB pipeline registers.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - uses pipeline register fields and comparators

examples.title: Pipeline Performance Calculator
examples.description: Program to calculate pipeline performance metrics including CPI, speedup, and execution time.
examples.explanation: This calculator demonstrates pipeline performance analysis. Key insight: speedup is limited by pipeline depth and hazard penalties. For large n, speedup approaches k (pipeline depth) but never exceeds it, demonstrating fundamental Amdahl's law limitations on parallel processing.
